issue_title: Optimization with recursive functions yield incorrect result
issue_body:
Thanks @NikolajBjorner Your recent fixes to recursive functions allowed me to make progress. Alas, I'm still seeing bizarre behavior.

This script:

```
(declare-datatypes  () ((Item (mk-item (size Int) (weight Int)))))
(declare-fun items  () (Array Int Item))
(declare-fun picked () (Array Int Bool))

(assert (= (select items 1) (mk-item 4 2)))
(assert (= (select items 2) (mk-item 4 2)))
(assert (= (select items 3) (mk-item 1 4)))
(assert (= (select items 4) (mk-item 5 5)))
(assert (= (select items 5) (mk-item 3 2)))
(assert (= (select items 6) (mk-item 1 9)))

(define-fun-rec computeSize ((lb Int) (ub Int)) Int
   (ite (> lb ub)
        0
        (+ (ite (select picked lb) (size (select items lb)) 0)
           (computeSize (+ lb 1) ub))))

(define-fun-rec computeWeight ((lb Int) (ub Int)) Int
   (ite (> lb ub)
        0
        (+ (ite (select picked lb) (weight (select items lb)) 0)
           (computeWeight (+ lb 1) ub))))

(define-fun totalSize   () Int (computeSize 1 6))
(define-fun totalWeight () Int (computeWeight 1 6))
(assert (< totalSize 10))
(maximize totalWeight)

(check-sat)
(get-value (totalSize))
(get-value (totalWeight))
(eval (select picked 1))
(eval (select picked 2))
(eval (select picked 3))
(eval (select picked 4))
(eval (select picked 5))
(eval (select picked 6))
```

produces:

```
sat
((totalSize 7))
((totalWeight 4))
false
true
false
false
true
false
```

Unfortunately, that is the incorrect answer. There is a solution such that the `totalWeight` is actually `18`, by picking items 3, 4, and 6.

When I code the whole thing without recursive functions, I get the correct answer indeed. In fact, if you remove:

```
(maximize totalWeight)
```

And replace it with:

```
(assert (>= totalWeight 18))
```

that also works as well, producing:

```
sat
((totalSize 7))
((totalWeight 18))
false
false
true
true
false
true
```

So, I'm not sure what to blame here; but seems to be an interaction between optimization and recursively defined functions.

issue_title: fp - float to Int64 conversion
issue_body:
The following benchmark uses an admittedly convoluted way of converting the single-precision float number `0.5` to a signed 64-bit value, using round-nearest-ties-to-even-mode:

```
(set-option :produce-models true)
(set-logic QF_FPBV)
(define-fun s2 () (_ FloatingPoint  8 24) ((_ to_fp 8 24) roundNearestTiesToEven (/ 1 2)))
(declare-fun s0 () (_ FloatingPoint  8 24))
(declare-fun s1 () (_ BitVec 64))
(assert
   (let ((s3 (fp.eq s0 s2)))
   (let ((s9 ((_ fp.to_sbv 64) roundNearestTiesToEven s0)))
   (let ((s12 (= (bvcomp s1 s9) #b1)))
   (let ((s13 (and s3 s12)))
   s13)))))
(check-sat)
(get-value (s1))
```

Z3 says:

```
sat
((s1 #x0000000000000001))
```

i.e., z3 converts the value to `1`. I believe the correct result should be `0` in this rounding-mode.

issue_title: SMTLib compliance: quotes for echo
issue_body:
Page 67 of http://smtlib.cs.uiowa.edu/papers/smt-lib-reference-v2.6-draft-3.pdf says:

> `(echo s)` where s is a string literal, simply prints back s as is—including the surrounding
> double-quotes.

Strictly speaking, z3 is violating this as it does not print the surrounding quotes. I think the motivation is that the output of all commands are valid s-expressions, and putting the quotes would ensure this invariant is always held.

Perhaps not the most important detail, but would be good to be compliant in this case as well.

issue_title: `--quiet` option should only silence the stdout handler
issue_body:
We need to change `CLILoader.option_process()` so that `--quiet` only affects the stdout handler, not all logging handlers:  https://github.com/level12/keg/blob/master/keg/cli.py#L335

The idea behind `--quiet` is that you can silence CLI output, and therefore avoid CRON email nose.  However, with the current implementation, all log handlers are affected, which can keep log messages from getting upstream (i.e. to Logzio, rsyslog, etc.).

issue_title: add batch-wise dropout layer, leaky ReLUs, ...
issue_body:
The results of the CIFAR-10 competition on Kaggle are out, and the 1st ranked solution uses some interesting tricks that we should probably make available in nntools: http://www.kaggle.com/c/cifar-10/forums/t/10493/train-you-very-own-deep-convolutional-network

**leaky ReLUs**
This idea has been proposed before, but I haven't really seen it used in the wild until now. Instead of having `y = max(0, x)`, you have `y = max(x/a, x)`, where a is some constant (say `a = 10` or `a = 3`). This means you still get some sort of nonlinearity, but the gradient can flow through in both regimes. I don't think we have a clean way to do parameterised nonlinearities yet, maybe we need to create a callable class for this, like we have for the initializers. We could just use nested functions, but that will cause issues with picklability of the models (see #7).

**batch-wise dropout**
Instead of sampling a random dropout mask for each sample, and multiplying the batch with the mask, sample only one dropout mask for the entire batch and index the batch and parameter matrices to completely delete all dropped out units. Between two layers with batch-wise dropout, the size of the dot product is reduced by 75%, so this could mean a speedup of up to 4x.

Of course this will also affect the learning trajectory, but I suspect it has advantages and disadvantages (it's probably not inherently worse than doing sample-wise dropout). I don't know if it will be feasible to reap the gains in computational efficiency in Theano, since implementing this will require advanced indexing (i.e. making copies). But it's worth implementing and evaluating, it doesn't sound too hard.

Unfortunately it probably isn't possible to isolate this into a separate layer like we did with regular dropout, since the parameter matrices have be to be indexed with the dropout mask.

issue_title: create docval macros for acceptable data types
issue_body:
When specifying a type for an argument in docval, we must list out (in a tuple) all acceptable types for the argument. For example, the `data` argument to `TimeSeries` takes three types.

```python
...
    {'name': 'data', 'type': (Iterable, 'TimeSeries', DataChunkIterator), 'doc': '...'}
...
```

to make it easier to maintain, we should be able to use something like this:

```python
...
    {'name': 'data', 'type': ('array', 'TimeSeries'), 'doc': '...'}
...
```

and docval will know `'array'` translates to `(Iterable, DataChunkIterator)`

issue_title: Spellcheck in markdown cells
issue_body:
This issue outlines a variety of thoughts on spellchecking in markdown cells in the notebook.

**Problem:** Spellchecking is currently unavailable in CodeMirror because as of CodeMirror version 4.0, CodeMirror elements are not recognized as editable content areas by the browser. As a result, the system spellchecker is not instantiated in those editable elements.

This problem is not unique to the Jupyter ecosystem and is also not unique to the CodeMirror editor.

**Solutions:**
- Implement a spellcheck extension using CodeMirror extensions and a spellcheck library like typo.js.
    - Pros: Can be used by other people in the CodeMirror ecosystem
    - Cons: Lots of maintenance overhead, hard to expand for multiple langagues, will change as CodeMirror API changes
- Use a separate editor for markdown cells
    - Pros: Don't have to worry about CodeMirror shennagins with spellcheck at all
    - Cons: Have to worry about more components being loaded on the page
        - This can be alleviated by rendering some components statically when they are not in use
    - Cons: Will make cross-cell interactions (like shifting cell focus) difficult as context has to be switched between two different types of components

I was initially leaning towards the first option, but really the second option seems more viable at this point. It limits the changes that we would have to make to our codebase only and helps us avoid having to build and maintain another library or tool (despite the fact that it is very much so needed in the CodeMirror ecosystem).

We can think about how to make the cell focus stuff play nicely with this as well since that has been a topic of thought.

issue_title: Evaluate operations on constants directly, without creating a RdTmp
issue_body:
Right now, the following code will fail:

```
    x = self.constant(42, Type.int_32)
    y = x + 1
    print y.rdt.con
```

This is because the `x + 1` creates a new `RdTmp`. It would be nice if we could evaluate constant expressions directly. One case where this is annoying is if you're computing jump targets for conditional jumps: vex only supports constant targets, so you have to make sure you never introduce any vex value into the computation or you'll get a RdTmp which is not constant.

issue_title: API explanations in the top level Vehicle or convenience classes?
issue_body:
If you have an attribute like ``vehicle.heading`` which represents a single value there is just one place where it can be documented.

For a compound object like ``Vehicle.location`` you can have the documentation in the top level attribute or spread in the sub classes like ``Locations``, ``LocationGlobal``, ``LocationGlobalRelative`` etc. 

Having most of the docs up in the top level ``vehicle`` makes sense because you mostly operate on the attributes through this object, but it does mean that you can't easily scan through ``Vehicle`` to see the available attributes and then dig down for functionality. This wouldn't matter so much if there was a class listing in the sidebar (as requested) but it is a bit of a pain as we don't.

Most of all though, it is important to try be consistent. My leaning is to have most of the documents in the top level ``Vehicle``, with minimal documentation in "sub attributes" (and hope our docs infrastructure improves).

@tcr3dr @mrpollo Do you have any opinion on this?

issue_title: Create playlist from a txt file
issue_body:
Hi!

I just managed to add my first feature to mps-youtube. Here's the usecase:

- I have list of songs copied from web into a file or I just enter a list of songs into a file manually.
- I want mpsyt to read that list, search yt and add them to current playlist

At the moment, it's still work in progress and status is "it works for me". Here are some constraints:

- One song per line
- Songs have to be prepended with numbers, like this: `128. some artist - some song`. Numeration will be striped
- No empty lines allowed
- The first match from results list will be used

This feature can be started with `fetch` command, followed by a file path to file containing list of songs. Limit for first n entries can be added, too. Example command:

```
fetch /tmp/songlist.txt 15
```

Code is here: https://github.com/Atlantic777/mps-youtube/commit/dbe78f47ac00e76fc3bcaa7833ea145ff1c20802

You know that it's working something when there is log output after each matched song.

I'm asking you for comments and suggestions. Please, test it, make it crash and share problematic examples. I would like to hear if this usecase is interesting to someone else. It works for me, but I'll try to make it work for you, too.

Here are some things on the *todo* list:
- Appropriate name for this command
- Better striping rule is needed, but I have to think about that a bit more.
- Some kind of song matcher should be used, too. In example. if song length is 60 minutes that's probably a miss. Maybe that filter can be skipped if entry contains word "album"  or something.
- Whole playlist compilation process takes a bit long. Maybe there is a way to trigger multiple search requests in parallel.

Please, share your thoughts on this. Feedback is priceless.

Thanks! :)

issue_title: proxy mode: add support for disabling head insert and banner
issue_body:
Proxy mode does not include `wombat.js`, however, the head insert is still added by default and the banner is still displayed. Add the following options, which default to true.

```
proxy:
    ...
    use_banner: true
    use_head_insert: true
```

- If `use_banner` is set to false, the banner in `banner.html` will not be added. (A custom banner can override this property, which is checked in the Jinja2 template)

- If `use_head_insert` is false, no modifications are made to the page, `head_insert.html` is not inserted at all.

issue_title: libopencv-dev broke with RealSense Samples Install
issue_body:
I broke libopencv-dev during my install of RealSense Samples for the R200. I wanted to install realsense_ros_camera. My goal was to use it with OpenCL Caffe, which at the time was working with my laptop's built in web cam. It appears that I should not have attempted this and should have moved on to newer RealSense camera instead. I attempted to roll back by uninstalling RealSense Samples but have been unable to recover libopencv-dev. Furthermore, I cannot install RZ300 drivers until libopencv-dev is fixed. 

I posted this on https://communities.intel.com/message/561462#561462. They sent me here. I am hoping this is an issue someone can solve here. If not I can try a ROS group. It appears to be an interaction between ROS and the RealSense SDK. 
 
Apologies: I'm afraid that my knowledge of Ubuntu allows me to get into trouble but not always out of it. Details follow.
 
- Ubuntu: 16.04
- ROS: Kinetic
- R200 (I will upgrade to a RZ300 after I fix libopencv-dev)
- Recent Dell Laptop

I believe I broke OpenCL Caffe and other applications during the  RealSense Samples. I suspect it happened during this command.

`sudo apt install -y librealsense-object-recognition-dev librealsense-persontracking-dev librealsense-slam-dev libopencv-dev`
 
This is what I discovered about my install.
```
$ aptitude search librealsense-object-recognition-dev
     u   librealsense-object-recognition     - Intel(R) RealSense(tm) Object Recognition
 
$ aptitude search librealsense-persontracking-dev
     u   librealsense-persontracking-dev     - Development files for Intel RealSense Pers
 
$ aptitude search librealsense-slam-dev
     u   librealsense-slam-dev               - Development files for Intel RealSense SLAM
 
$ aptitude search libopencv-dev
     BB  libopencv-dev                       - development files for opencv             
     p   libopencv-dev:i386                  - development files for opencv

``` 
I attempted to back out by removing all the packages I installed above with:
sudo apt-get remove <package-name>
 
Then I reinstalled libopencv-dev. It failed when it tried to unpack libopencv3.1-java.
 
```
sudo apt-get install libopencv-dev
...
Unpacking libopencv3.1-java (3.1.0+dfsg-1~realsense4.18) ...
dpkg: error processing archive /var/cache/apt/archives/libopencv3.1-java_3.1.0+dfsg-1~realsense4.18_all.deb (--unpack):
trying to overwrite '/usr/share/java/opencv.jar', which is also in package libopencv2.4-java 2.4.9.1+dfsg-1.5ubuntu1
...
```
In retrospect it appears to be trying to install libopencv-dev 3.1 over 2.4.9 and couldn't. I don't fully understand what the problem is and did a lot of unwise things while I was trying to figure it out. I am no longer sure what my opencv status is. I understand that ROS can be touchy when it comes to specific package versions. On my other Ubuntu box it has ros-kinetic-opencv3, libopencv-dev, and libopencv2.4-java installed. I think I want to get back to that state and don't know how.
 
This is my current status when I attempt to install libopencv-dev.
```
-- BEFORE libopencv-dev INSTALL
  $ aptitude search libopencv2.4-java
    p   libopencv2.4-java               - Java bindings for the computer vision libr
 
  $ aptitude search libopencv3.1-java
    p   libopencv3.1-java               - Java bindings for the computer vision libr
 
  $ aptitude search libopencv-dev
    p   libopencv-dev                   - development files for opencv             
    p   libopencv-dev:i386              - development files for opencv             
 
-- DURING libopencv-dev INSTALL
  $ sudo apt-get install libopencv-dev
    [sudo] password for xxxx:
    Reading package lists... Done
    Building dependency tree      
    Reading state information... Done
    The following packages were automatically installed and are no longer required:
      linux-headers-4.15.0-24 linux-headers-4.15.0-24-generic
      linux-headers-4.15.0-29 linux-headers-4.15.0-29-generic
      linux-headers-4.4.0-130 linux-headers-4.4.0-130-generic
      linux-image-4.15.0-24-generic linux-image-4.15.0-29-generic
      linux-modules-4.15.0-24-generic linux-modules-4.15.0-29-generic
    Use 'sudo apt autoremove' to remove them.
    The following additional packages will be installed:
      libopencv3.1-java
    The following NEW packages will be installed:
       libopencv-dev libopencv3.1-java
    0 upgraded, 2 newly installed, 0 to remove and 37 not upgraded.
    Need to get 0 B/487 kB of archives.
    After this operation, 849 kB of additional disk space will be used.
    Do you want to continue? [Y/n] Y
    Selecting previously unselected package libopencv3.1-java.
     (Reading database ... 512825 files and directories currently installed.)
    Preparing to unpack .../libopencv3.1-java_3.1.0+dfsg-1~realsense4.18_all.deb ...
    Unpacking libopencv3.1-java (3.1.0+dfsg-1~realsense4.18) ...
    Selecting previously unselected package libopencv-dev.
    Preparing to unpack .../libopencv-dev_3.1.0+dfsg-1~realsense4.18_amd64.deb ...
    Unpacking libopencv-dev (3.1.0+dfsg-1~realsense4.18) ...
    Processing triggers for man-db (2.7.5-1) ...
    Setting up libopencv3.1-java (3.1.0+dfsg-1~realsense4.18) ...
    Setting up libopencv-dev (3.1.0+dfsg-1~realsense4.18) ...
 
-- AFTER libopencv-dev INSTALL
  $ aptitude search libopencv-dev
    iB  libopencv-dev                   - development files for opencv             
    p   libopencv-dev:i386              - development files for opencv
            
  $ aptitude search libopencv3.1-java
    iBA libopencv3.1-java               - Java bindings for the computer vision libr
 
  $ aptitude search libopencv2.4-java
    p   libopencv2.4-java               - Java bindings for the computer vision libr
 
  $ aptitude search opencv | grep ^i
    ...
    i A ros-kinetic-opencv3             - OpenCV 3.x
 
  $ find /opt/ros/kinetic/ -name cv2.so
    /opt/ros/kinetic/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/cv2.so
```
I don't understand what "iB" means in the above context.

As I stated above, I believe I want to get back to version 2.4.9. So that a package search will include the following. I am confused by the mixture of versions 2.4 and 3.x in my package searches but this is how it looks on my working Ubuntu system.

 ```
-- TARGET OF WHAT I THINK I WANT
  $ aptitude search opencv | grep ^i
    i   libopencv-dev                   - development files for opencv
    i A libopencv2.4-java               - Java bindings for the computer vision libr
    i A ros-kinetic-opencv3             - openCV 3.x
    ...
 
  $ find /opt/ros/kinetic/ -name cv2.so
    /opt/ros/kinetic/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/cv2.so
``` 
I am appreciative of any clues about how to further analyze my problem and recover. I suspect you will need additional information.
 
My goal is to leverage the IntelROSProject (http://wiki.ros.org/IntelROSProject).

issue_title: Sync frame and understanding frame rates, frame numbers and timestamps
issue_body:
| Required Info                         |                                                                |
|---------------------------------|------------------------------------------- |
| Camera Model                       | { SR300 / D4150 } | 
| Firmware Version                   | 3.21.0.0 (SR300)/ | 
| Operating System & Version |   Linux (Ubuntu 16.04 | 
| Kernel Version (Linux Only)    |  4.4.0-116-generic                                         | 
| Platform                                 | PC  |
| SDK Version                            |  2.10.0            | 


### Issue Description
I've captured frame data, hardware timestamps, time of arrival timestamps and frame numbers, and something seems strange. While I've configured the camera with the following settings:
```
        cfg.enable_stream(RS2_STREAM_COLOR, 1920, 1080, RS2_FORMAT_RGB8, 30);
        cfg.enable_stream(RS2_STREAM_DEPTH, 640, 480, RS2_FORMAT_Z16, 30);
        ...
        pipe_.start(config)
```
The frame numbers and timestamps from the depth and color frames, seem to show that the rgb frame rate its almost 1/4 (~5 fps) of the depth sensor, could that be right (check the data bellow)?

Is it possible that I'm doing something wrong? This is the way I'm collecting the data:
```
		rs2::frameset data = pipe_.wait_for_frames();

		rs2::video_frame color = data.get_color_frame();
		rs2::depth_frame depth = data.get_depth_frame();

                print_frame_info(color, *log_file);
                print_frame_info(depth, *log_file);
                (*log_file) << std::endl;

```

issue_title: Test the `repoview` option for the yum_distributor
issue_body:
`repoview` is an option which can be enabled in the config for the `yum_distributor`, it requires the `generate_sqlite` option to be enabled, because sqlite files are used by the repoview tool.
If `repoview` is enabled, there will be an additional step responsible for the generation of the HTML files during publish.
As for the browsing, the HTTP request will be redirected to show a repoview content if it is available.

If one will enable `repoview` via pulp-admin, the `generate_sqlite` option will be silently enabled as well. If one would like to use REST API to enable `repoview`, the `generate_sqlite` option should be specified explicitly, otherwise an error will occur.

Suggestion for the test:
- create RPM repo
- sync and publish it
- observe no redirect and the directory list of packages when accessing repo content via HTTP(S).
- update `yum_distributor` with `repoview` and `generate_sqlite` options set to `True`
- re-publish repo
- observe redirect (302) to repoview content: https://path_to_the_repo/repoview/index.html

Redmine story: https://pulp.plan.io/issues/189
Redmine issue: https://pulp.plan.io/issues/2001

issue_title: Remove all Python 2 related compatibility
issue_body:
On #332 we agreed that we are able to drop Python 2 support for Pulp Smash. Here is the plan to actually get that done:

- [ ] Drop the compat module
- [ ] Update the imports to reflect the removal of the compat module
- [ ] Drop unittest2 in favor of Python 3's unitttest module and update the code
- [ ] Drop mock in favor of the builtin Python 3's mock
- [ ] Update the `setup.py` file
- [ ] Update the documentation

issue_title: deprecation_reason not works for Mutation.Argument
issue_body:

When I set `deprecation_reason` on a Argument, it raises an `TypeError` and says `deprecation_reason` is a unexpected keyword argument.

Environment:
Mac/Docker container with Ubuntu image.
python: 3.6.5
graphene: 2.1.2

Reproduce snippet:

```Python
import graphene


class CreateSomething(graphene.Mutation):

    class Arguments:
        deprecated_id = graphene.ID(deprecation_reason='deprecated')

    ok = graphene.Boolean()

    @staticmethod
    def mutate(root, *_, **__):
        return CreateSomething(ok=True)


class RootMutation(graphene.ObjectType):
    create_something = CreateSomething.Field()


schema = graphene.Schema(mutation=RootMutation)

query = '''
mutation createSomething($deprecatedId: ID) {
    createSomething(deprecatedId: $deprecatedId) {
        ok
    }
}
'''
result = schema.execute(
    query, variable_values={
        'deprecatedId': '123',
    })
print('result:', result.data)

```

result: OrderedDict([('createSomething', OrderedDict([('ok', True)]))])

issue_title: Thumbnail rendering too slow (1s per thumbnail)
issue_body: I'm working with the latest version of sorl-thumbnail (12.4a1) in a distributed system and the rendering is way too slow, around 1s/thumbnail. ##### Software used - sorl-thumbnail 12.4a1 - Python 3.5 - Django 1.11.3 - Google Cloud Storage - Redis 4.0 (through a Unix socket) - Ubuntu 17.04 (GNU/Linux 4.10.0-32-generic x86_64). ##### Expected behavior The thumbnail rendering should be practically instantaneous if cached. ##### Actual behavior The thumbnail rendering when cached takes around 1 second _(0.5-1.5s)_. ##### Steps to reproduce the behavior ``` {% thumbnail user.image.image as img %} {% empty %} {% endthumbnail %} ``` ##### Additional info I've identified a few problems: **1) Line 153: `output = self.nodelist_file.render(context)` takes ~1 second to render** ``` Timer unit: 1e-06 s Total time: 0.604442 s File: /Users/[...]/lib/python3.6/site-packages/sorl/thumbnail/templatetags/thumbnail.py Function: _render at line 129 Line # Hits Time Per Hit % Time Line Contents ============================================================== 129 @profile_each_line 130 def _render(self, context): 131 1 53 53.0 0.0 file_ = self.file_.resolve(context) 132 1 6 6.0 0.0 geometry = self.geometry.resolve(context) 133 1 0 0.0 0.0 options = {} 134 3 3 1.0 0.0 for key, expr in self.options: 135 2 2 1.0 0.0 noresolve = {'True': True, 'False': False, 'None': None} 136 2 13 6.5 0.0 value = noresolve.get(text_type(expr), expr.resolve(context)) 137 2 2 1.0 0.0 if key == 'options': 138 options.update(value) 139 else: 140 2 2 1.0 0.0 options[key] = value 141 142 1 1832 1832.0 0.3 thumbnail = get_thumbnail(file_, geometry, **options) 143 144 1 2 2.0 0.0 if not thumbnail or (isinstance(thumbnail, DummyImageFile) and self.nodelist_empty): 145 if self.nodelist_empty: 146 return self.nodelist_empty.render(context) 147 else: 148 return '' 149 150 1 2 2.0 0.0 if self.as_var: 151 1 9 9.0 0.0 context.push() 152 1 3 3.0 0.0 context[self.as_var] = thumbnail 153 1 602506 602506.0 99.7 output = self.nodelist_file.render(context) 154 1 6 6.0 0.0 context.pop() 155 else: 156 output = thumbnail.url 157 158 1 1 1.0 0.0 return output ``` **2) The class `ImageFile(BaseImageFile)` from sorl.thumbnail.images takes between 0.5-1 second if used with Google Cloud Storage from _django-storages_ due to the initialization of `self.storage`** This lag is especially noticeable in this section of the previously mentioned class: ``` if hasattr(self.storage, 'location'): location = self.storage.location if not self.storage.location.endswith("/"): location += "/" if self.name.startswith(location): self.name = self.name[len(location):] ```

issue_title: Run-tag updates are not atomic
issue_body:
We instantiate scalar charts as

```html
<tf-scalar-chart tag="[[item.tag]]" runs="[[item.runs]]">
</tf-scalar-chart>.
```

An observer on `tf-scalar-chart` fires each time that its `tag` or
`runs` properties change.

If `item` changes from `{tag: tag1, runs: runs1}` to
`{tag: tag2, runs: tag2}`, then Polymer will first update one of the
properties, then fire the observer, then update the other property and
fire the observer. As a result, at one point the observer is fired when
the component has an inconsistent state.

That is, Polymer takes our atomic updates and makes them non-atomic.

This manifests as 500s of the form, "Key `thing` was not found in
Reservoir," corresponding to urls of the form
`/scalar?tag=tag1&run=run2`, where `run2` is not a valid run for `tag1`.
For instance:

    Error: RequestNetworkError: 500 at data/plugin/scalars/scalars?run=mnist_with_summaries%2Ftest&tag=frequency

(Here, `frequency` comes from the audio dataset, not the MNIST dataset.)

Proposed solution: let `tf-scalar-chart` depend on `item` itself.

issue_title: Show version string in TensorBoard UI
issue_body:
In https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorboard/issues/150, user had an issue that was made more confusing by the fact that they were actually hitting a stale cache and getting an older version of TensorBoard.

We can make this clearer by presenting the version string in the TensorBoard UI. We could have the core plugin serve a route for this. If we want to avoid the caching issue, we should find a way to inject it into index.html; if we just make a new route served by the core plugin, then possibly the user would get a stale index, but the new route. (Alternatively, we could serve the version string from a route, but ensure it has the same caching policy as index.html? Not quite as robust, but quite possibly easier for us to maintain.)

Actually - I wonder if we could use a BUILD time dependency to get the version string into the UI? @jart @wchargin thoughts?

issue_title: `send_and_recv` performance issue
issue_body:
https://github.com/python-xlib/python-xlib/blob/63d5cf15901ec945ac45c6a58b58d12b491e5a9b/Xlib/protocol/display.py#L593

This simple program takes 10+ secs to run:

    from Xlib import display, X

    def main():
        win = display.Display().screen().root
        win.get_image(0, 0, 1280, 720, X.ZPixmap, 0xffffffff)

    for i in range(10):
        main()

Consider using a large recv buffer as it will drastically increase performance. For example, if you use 2M instead of 2K buffer then the same program can have 20x speed boost.

issue_title: Support "granular" transaction creation steps
issue_body:
Creating a transaction via `BigchainDB.transactions.create` involves three main steps:
1. Creation of the dictionary representing the transaction for the given user, and data payload.
2. Signature of the transaction, resulting in a new dictionary
3. Broadcast the transaction to one or more BigchainDB server nodes.

The above three steps are not directly available via the bigchaindb driver, and it is the goal of this ticket to make them available.

issue_title: MySQL error, Boolean insert receiving strings instead of literals
issue_body:
For a CSV that has a boolean field `old_layout` (for example) where the CSV row values are always either "true" or "false", csvsql helpfully creates a table schema with a column type of `tinyint(1)` (as aliased by `BOOL`). But when it tries to insert the CSV data into that table, it is trying to use the strings "true" and "false" instead of the Boolean literals that will be accepted as aliases for 1 and 0. 

The MySQL error returned by csvkit is `Incorrect integer value: 'true' for column 'old_layout' at row 1"` and the element being used inside the `VALUES` clause is `u'true'`.

The command I'm running is `csvsql -v my.csv --tables my_table --db mysql://root@localhost/my_db --insert --no-create --datetime-format "%m/%d/%Y %I:%M %p" --blank --no-inference`

Is this something about how an underlying library is behaving, or something that could be addressed within csvkit? MySQL version is `14.14 Distrib 5.7.19, for osx10.12 (x86_64)` if that helps.

issue_title: Tip: Pipe multiple files into csvjoin
issue_body:
Hey guys, I noticed in [the docs](http://csvkit.readthedocs.org/en/540/tricks.html#specifying-stdin-as-a-file) a tip about piping a file to commands like `csvjoin`. In my case, I wanted to pipe the results of multiple streams into `csvjoin` for a [data workflow](https://github.com/CityOfPhiladelphia/property-assessments-data-pipeline), so I did some digging and came across [named pipes](http://www.linuxjournal.com/article/2156). For example, if you want to cut 2 files before joining them:

```bash
csvjoin -c "id,id" <(csvcut -c 2,5,6 file_a.csv) <(csvcut -c 1,7 file_b.csv)
```
Thought it might be handy to mention in the tricks section if you're interested.

https://github.com/scikit-learn-contrib/imbalanced-learn/blob/eafae67994476a0c239bf459d5ea5d875ef9f3f1/imblearn/pipeline.py#L349-L350 vs.
https://github.com/scikit-learn-contrib/imbalanced-learn/blob/eafae67994476a0c239bf459d5ea5d875ef9f3f1/imblearn/pipeline.py#L367-L368

The behaviour appears to be correct (sampling should only be applied when fitting) but the docstrings are misleading.

#### Affected Methods
* predict
* predict_proba
* decision_function
* predict_log_proba
* score

issue_title: Compile time error with bindings when using private inheritance
issue_body:
## Issue description

Bindings to a derived class with private inheritance results in a compile time error in 2.2.2. Using the deprecated `py::base<T>()` attribute does not give a compile time error. 

### Error Log

```/usr/local/include/python2.7/pybind11/pybind11.h: In instantiation of ‘pybind11::class_<type_, options>::add_base(pybind11::detail::type_record&)::<lambda(void*)> [with Base = Base; typename std::enable_if<std::integral_constant<bool, (std::is_base_of<T, type_>::value && (! std::is_same<T, type_>::value))>::value, int>::type <anonymous> = 0; type_ = Derived; options = {Base}]’:
/usr/local/include/python2.7/pybind11/pybind11.h:1075:37:   required from ‘struct pybind11::class_<type_, options>::add_base(pybind11::detail::type_record&) [with Base = Base; typename std::enable_if<std::integral_constant<bool, (std::is_base_of<T, type_>::value && (! std::is_same<T, type_>::value))>::value, int>::type <anonymous> = 0; type_ = Derived; options = {Base}]::<lambda(void*)>’
/usr/local/include/python2.7/pybind11/pybind11.h:1075:9:   required from ‘static void pybind11::class_<type_, options>::add_base(pybind11::detail::type_record&) [with Base = Base; typename std::enable_if<std::integral_constant<bool, (std::is_base_of<T, type_>::value && (! std::is_same<T, type_>::value))>::value, int>::type <anonymous> = 0; type_ = Derived; options = {Base}]’
/usr/local/include/python2.7/pybind11/pybind11.h:1060:9:   required from ‘pybind11::class_<type_, options>::class_(pybind11::handle, const char*, const Extra& ...) [with Extra = {}; type_ = Derived; options = {Base}]’
src/main.cpp:20:43:   required from here
/usr/local/include/python2.7/pybind11/pybind11.h:1076:69: error: ‘Base’ is an inaccessible base of ‘Derived’
             return static_cast<Base *>(reinterpret_cast<type *>(src));
```

## Reproducible example code

```
#include <pybind11/pybind11.h>                                                  
                                                                                
namespace py = pybind11;                                                        
                                                                                
class Base {                                                                    
  public:                                                                       
    void f1() {}                                                                
};                                                                              
                                                                                
class Derived : private Base {

PYBIND11_MODULE(python_example, m) {                                            
    m.doc() = "test";

issue_title: renewer does not use full path when accessing config files
issue_body:
`os.listdir` returns basenames only, not full paths:
https://github.com/letsencrypt/letsencrypt/blob/master/letsencrypt/renewer.py#L171

This makes the renewer only work if it is run from the `/etc/letsencrypt/renewal` directory. `cli_config.renewal_configs_dir` needs to be prepended to `renewal_file`.

issue_title: Standardize how you’ll run mypy
issue_body:
From http://blog.zulip.org/2016/10/13/static-types-in-python-oh-mypy/:

Write tooling to [install](https://github.com/zulip/zulip/blob/master/tools/install-mypy) and [run](https://github.com/zulip/zulip/blob/master/tools/run-mypy) `mypy` against your codebase, so that everyone using the project can run the type checker the same way. Two features are important in how you run mypy:

- Support for determining which files should be checked (a whitelist/exclude list is useful!).
- Specifying the correct flags for your project at this time. For a Python 2 project, I recommend starting with `mypy --py2 --silent-imports --fast-parser -i <paths>`. You should be able to do this using a [mypy.ini](http://mypy.readthedocs.io/en/latest/config_file.html) file.

issue_body:
Hi, I work for a host provider and I'm trying to use certbot. 

I am using the following command:
certbot certonly --webroot -w /home/{domain}/public -n -q -m {email} -d {domain} --agree-tos --cert-path /etc/letsencrypt/archive/{domain} --key-path /etc/letsencrypt/archive/{domain} --chain-path /etc/letsencrypt/archive/{domain} --fullchain-path /etc/letsencrypt/archive/{domain}

My problem is that I haven't control over my customers' htaccess content, and many of them have some rules that block access to folders and files starting with dot (e.g .well-known). This is making fail the domain validation.

issue_title: Curious Warning : Ring Self-intersection at or near point 0 1
Ensure user has enough ether left in the account to safely interact with the netting channel
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

If a uniformed user creates a netting channel with all it's ether then she will not be able to close/updateTransfer/settle it to recover it's asset.

## Solution

This is a hard problem and would required monitoring of the account's balance, an initial solution should ensure during the netting channel creation that the account's balance is sufficient to complete the close/updateTransfer + settle operations.

## Tasklist

Preliminary list of tasks (split into multiple issues)

- [ ] Add a configuration variable for the minimum number of pending transfer
- [ ] Ensure the account will has enough balance to call close/updateTransfer + the minimum pending transfer unlocks + settle

- [ ] Add the limit in the Raiden client

issue_title: Include parentheses around expressions in token range
issue_body:
I am trying to use `asttokens` for source-level transformations in a code editor. For example, I would like to position the cursor on a binary operation, press a key, and the operands would be transposed: `(a+b) * c` → `c * (a+b)`.

The way I’m trying to do that is:

1. Parse a small portion of the file into an `asttokens.ASTTokens`. (The smaller the better because the whole file cannot be guaranteed to be syntactically correct all the time. For now, I operate at the level of function definitions.)
2. Find the target node by the cursor position. Check if it’s a `BinOp`.
3. Determine the character ranges corresponding to the `left` and `right` operands, and swap them. (This is to preserve as much of the original code formatting as possible. For instance, re-generating the whole function using `astor` will strip all comments and possibly mess up spacing and line structure.)

However, I find that at step 3, if either operand is enclosed in parentheses, they do not count towards the token range:

```python
import asttokens

tokens = asttokens.ASTTokens('(a + b) * c', parse=True)
print('\n'.join(
    '# {} at {}: {!r}'.format(node.__class__.name,
                              tokens.get_text_range(node),
                              tokens.get_text(node))
    for node in asttokens.util.walk(tokens.tree))

# Module at (0, 11): '(a + b) * c'
# Expr at (0, 11): '(a + b) * c'
# BinOp at (0, 11): '(a + b) * c'
# BinOp at (1, 6): 'a + b'
# Name at (1, 2): 'a'
# Name at (5, 6): 'b'
# Name at (10, 11): 'c'
```

so if I swap the corresponding character ranges, I get `(c) * a + b` which is wrong.

It would be nice if any parentheses that are not part of the parent node’s syntax were included in the token range of the child nodes:

```
# Module at (0, 11): '(a + b) * c'
# Expr at (0, 11): '(a + b) * c'
# BinOp at (0, 11): '(a + b) * c'
# BinOp at (0, 7): '(a + b)'
# Name at (1, 2): 'a'
# Name at (5, 6): 'b'
# Name at (10, 11): 'c'
```

(Or am I overlooking some other Obvious Way to Do It?)

issue_title: Tagging tasks
issue_body:
Related to #486, I think it is helpful to **tag** tasks with keywords, something like

1. When a task is created, it is automatically tagged with workflow name, step name, index etc.
2. Tag does not affect the creation of IDs so tasks with the same content still refer to the same task (ID).
3. Tags are displayed with `sos status` etc
4. Tags can be used to submit, kill, resume tasks in batch.

Proposed interface:

1. `task: tags='horn'`: adding specified tag(s) to the tag list.
2. `sos status`: display tags
3. `sos status -t horn`: display all tasks with tag `horn`
4. `sos kill -t default_2`: kill all tasks submitted by step `default_2`

issue_title: Hide main functionality behind a flag
issue_body:
I'm debating whether it would be wise to require the detection to be behind a flag, the same way that after all is behind a flag, and that any other feature would also be behind a flag. Magic is best used sparingly and judiciously.

There are some places that I think magic is the only thing that makes sense. In particular, our hacks, of which there is currently only one, that allows the pypy3 pre-releases to work on Travis. There's no sense in doing that less magically.

The argument for the general detection behavior being behind a flag, however, is that it allows tox to run the same on Travis as it does locally. It's easy enough to add a flag in `tox_addoption`. Explicit opt-in allows users to decide exactly what they do (and don't) want overridden.

A future case where this might play out is if someone wishes to Tox-Travis for its ability to report back to GitHub (#73), but want to do all of that in a single job. It really shouldn't require a bunch of extra configuration work to do what Tox does by default.

An alternative would be a flag to _disable_ detection. That would be a backward compatible change, and would give the needed functionality, but it would have the flag being backward from rest of the features, which are (and are going to be) opt-in.

issue_title: How to use factory boy to test SQLalchemy association-object models?
issue_body:
Hi, I'm using [SQLalchemy association-object pattern](http://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/rel_1_1/orm/basic_relationships.html#association-object)  in my app and trying to figure out how to avoid recursion depth errors when testing it.  [I've posted a bounty question on stackoverflow](https://stackoverflow.com/q/45156782/1493069). How would one go about successfully testing this with factory-boy while avoiding the Recursion depth errors?  

Below are the three models that are working fine as needed in the app. 

```
class MemberOrgsAssoc(Model):
        """The left side of the relationship maps a User as a one-to-many to
        Organizations. User-Organization relevant data is stored in 
        this association-object table. Then, there is a one-to-many from
        this association-object table to the Organization table. """

        __tablename__ = 'member_orgs'

        member_id = Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey("users.id"), primary_key=True)
        org_id = Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey("organizations.id"), primary_key=True)
        manager_id = Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey("users.id"))
        org_title = Column(db.Unicode(50))
        organization = relationship("Organization", back_populates="members")
        member = relationship("User", back_populates="organizations",
                              foreign_keys=[member_id])
        manager = relationship("User", back_populates="subordinates",
                               foreign_keys=[manager_id])
```
```
class User(SurrogatePK, Model):
    """A user of the app."""
    __tablename__ = 'users'

    username = Column(db.Unicode(80), unique=True, nullable=False)
    organizations = relationship("MemberOrgsAssoc", back_populates="member",
                                 primaryjoin = "member_orgs.c.member_id == User.id",
                                 lazy="dynamic")
    subordinates = relationship("MemberOrgsAssoc", back_populates="manager",
                                primaryjoin = "member_orgs.c.manager_id == User.id",
                                lazy="dynamic")
```
```
class Organization(SurrogatePK, Model):
    """An organization that Users may belong to."""
    __tablename__ = 'organizations'
    name = Column(db.Unicode(128), nullable=False)
    members = relationship("MemberOrgsAssoc", back_populates="organization")
```

And here are the factories:

```
class MemberOrgsAssocFactory(BaseFactory):
    """Association-object table Factory"""

    class Meta:
        """Factory config"""
        model = MemberOrgsAssoc

    member_id = factory.SubFactory('tests.factories.UserFactory')
    org_id = factory.SubFactory('tests.factories.OrganizationFactory')
    manager_id = factory.SubFactory('tests.factories.UserFactory')
    org_title = Sequence(lambda n: 'CEO{0}'.format(n))
    organization = factory.SubFactory('tests.factories.OrganizationFactory')
    member = factory.SubFactory('tests.factories.UserFactory')
    manager = factory.SubFactory('tests.factories.UserFactory')
```
```
class UserFactory(BaseFactory):
    """User factory."""

    class Meta:
        """Factory configuration."""
        model = User

    username = Sequence(lambda n: 'user{0}'.format(n))
    organizations = factory.List(
        [factory.SubFactory('tests.factories.MemberOrgsAssocFactory')])
    subordinates = factory.List(
        [factory.SubFactory('tests.factories.MemberOrgsAssocFactory')])
```
```
class OrganizationFactory(BaseFactory):
    """Company factory"""

    class Meta:
        """Factory config"""
        model = Organization

    id = Sequence(lambda n: '{0}'.format(n))
    name = Sequence(lambda n: 'company{0}'.format(n))
    members = factory.List(
        [factory.SubFactory('tests.factories.MemberOrgsAssocFactory')])
```

Finally, here is the relevant pytest fixture:

```
@pytest.fixture(scope='function')
def user(db):
    """An user for the unit tests.
    setup reference: https://github.com/FactoryBoy/factory_boy/issues/101
    # how to handle self referential foreign key relation in factory boy
    # https://github.com/FactoryBoy/factory_boy/issues/173
    """
    user = UserFactory(
        organizations__0=None,
        subordinates__0=None,
    )

    a = MemberOrgsAssocFactory(
        is_org_admin=True,
        is_default_org=True,
        is_active=True,
    )

    a.organization=OrganizationFactory()
    admin_user.organizations.append(a)

    db.session.commit()
    return user
```

issue_title: Accept path-like objects for file uploads
issue_body:
Under `canvasapi` 0.7.0, `canvasapi.upload.Uploader.__init__` allows two ways to identify the file to be uploaded: it may be given as a string filename, or as an already-open file object. However, there are other ways to describe paths to files besides just strings: what the Python documentation calls “[path-like objects](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-path-like-object).” For example:

- From Python 3.4 onward, [the `pathlib` module](https://docs.python.org/3/library/pathlib.html#pathlib.PurePath) provides a rich API for manipulating filesystem paths as path-like objects rather than as strings.

- From Python 3.6 onward, the [`os.DirEntry`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/os.html#os.DirEntry) objects returned by [`os.scandir`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/os.html#os.scandir) are path-like objects.

As far as I can tell, the `Uploader` class will not behave correctly if constructed with a path-like object as its `file` argument. This should be improved so that path-like objects are treated like openable filenames, rather than being assumed to be already-open files.

<hr/>

Python 3.6 added several library features to make it easier to write code that transparently handles both string paths as well as path-like objects:

- A new [`os.PathLike`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/os.html#os.PathLike) abstract base class makes it possible to recognize path-like objects in a generic way, rather than checking for specific concrete classes.

- A new [`os.fspath`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/os.html#os.fspath) function returns the string representation of a path-like object, or simply returns its argument unchanged if that argument is already a string.

- Improvements to [`os.open`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/os.html#os.open) and other standard library functions allow them to accept either path-like objects or strings as arguments.

The Python 3.6 release notes include [more information on how to use these features](https://docs.python.org/3/whatsnew/3.6.html#pep-519-adding-a-file-system-path-protocol).

Unfortunately, those Python 3.6 improvements still leave a gap: Python 3.4 and 3.5 have the `pathlib` module, but do not have `os.PathLike`, `os.fspath`, the improvements to `os.open`, etc. Supporting path-like objects in these earlier Python releases may be inconvenient, or perhaps not worth doing at all.

to set your account's default identity.
Omit --global to set the identity only in this repository.

```

excerpted from https://travis-ci.org/danielballan/photomosaic/builds/175131428#L803

issue_title: Providers can only have a single set of configuration applied to them
issue_body:
Today, our model does not provide a way to have multiple instances of the same provider that are initialized with different configuration (for example, you can not have two aws providers, each targeting a different region).

The workaround today is to just run your Pulumi program multiple times with different sets of configurations (which you could, for example, vary by stack) but it's not 100% satisfying.  What we have is good enough to launch with, however, but we will probably need to do something here over time.

issue_title: False-positives in our "looks like a config secret" logic
issue_body:
Our integration tests in the Service repo found a couple of false positives in our logic to warn if a configuration value looks like a secret.

```
error: config value ''timer app created by "travis" at 2018-08-08 17:33:09.321234916 +0000 UTC m=+30.634475880'' looks like a secret; rerun with --secret to encrypt it, or --plaintext if you meant to store in plaintext
```

A lower priority false positive would be our AWS Access Key. My understanding is the AWS Secret Access Key is the sensitive part of the keypair, the Access Key ID is OK to expose.

Also, looking at the error, we are printing the errant log message with two `'` symbols.

issue_title: Is there a way to show ticket priority to staff only and not users?
Hide default fields
issue_body:
**Thanks for the awesome work here!** It would be great to see progress on the public facing web interface as well as the email system. I may do some work on this in the near future.

I noticed that there is currently no easy way to hide default fields... for our use case we wanted to hide the `due_date`, `priority`, and `assigned_to` fields from non-staff users. I ended up achieving that with the following patch. However some view code relies on the existence of the fields in the fields dictionary, so this is not complete as a PR.

Luckily the field expected by the view was the `assigned_to` field which has it's own special snowflake setting: `HELPDESK_CREATE_TICKET_HIDE_ASSIGNED_TO`

```
diff --git a/helpdesk/forms.py b/helpdesk/forms.py
index 7ab2024..d1b6994 100644
--- a/helpdesk/forms.py
+++ b/helpdesk/forms.py
@@ -317,6 +317,8 @@ class TicketForm(AbstractTicketForm):
         """
         super(TicketForm, self).__init__(*args, **kwargs)
         self._add_form_custom_fields()
+        for f in getattr(settings, 'EA_HIDDEN_TICKET_FIELDS', []):
+            self.fields.pop(f, None)
 
     def save(self, user=None):
         """
@@ -369,6 +371,8 @@ class PublicTicketForm(AbstractTicketForm):
         """
         super(PublicTicketForm, self).__init__(*args, **kwargs)
         self._add_form_custom_fields(False)
+        for f in getattr(settings, 'EA_PUBLIC_HIDDEN_TICKET_FIELDS', []):
+            self.fields.pop(f, None)
```

been suggested/reported

### Type:

- [x] Suggestion
- [ ] Bug

### Brief description of the suggestion
I would like the ability to set the token with a flag.
### What could it be useful for?
What someone could do is put the bot on GitHub. They would create a script that clones the repo, puts in the token, and uses it while running and push it to GitHub. When they are finished the token would get removed. The reason why someone might do this is if they share the bot with friends. It would be easier to share configs, edited code, etc. without worrying about the token being shown to the public.
### What would I use it for?
The same as what it could be useful for.

issue_title: BUG: Infinite inheritance
issue_body:
Hello.

## Bug
 You use `super(self.__class__, self).__init__()` in `Bot.py`. 

## Why it is bad
That is actually very bad, because of that issue: http://stackoverflow.com/questions/4235078/how-to-avoid-infinite-recursion-with-super. No one can inherit from Bot in cause of that issue.

## Solution:
Replace `super(self.__class__, self).__init__()` by `super(Bot.__init__, self).__init__()` or just` super()` for python 3

issue_title: Add delete functionality to the CLI
issue_body:
## User story
As a user of the DCP CLI, I want to have access to the bundle deletion functionality in the data store project so that I can delete bundles.

Depends on https://github.com/HumanCellAtlas/data-store/pull/684

## Context
The `/bundles/{uuid}` HTTP endpoint in `data-store` now supports the HTTP DELETE method. This method requires the `replica` to be specified in the query string.

* Triggering this without a version deletes the whole bundle
* Triggering this with the version deletes that specific version

issue_title: Inconsistent licence files
issue_body:
Following https://github.com/openjournals/joss-reviews/issues/268#issuecomment-318916583

In 
https://github.com/python-windrose/windrose/blob/master/setup.py
it's `License :: OSI Approved :: CEA CNRS Inria Logiciel Libre License, version 2.1 (CeCILL-2.1)'`

but in https://github.com/python-windrose/windrose/blob/master/LICENCE
and in https://github.com/python-windrose/windrose/blob/master/windrose/version.py#L5
it's CeCILL-B

In https://pypi.python.org/pypi?%3Aaction=list_classifiers
CeCILL-2.1 is the only CeCILL license (moreover it's OSI approved)
so if we change to use CeCILL-B everywhere in this project we need to know what should be written in `setup.py` (maybe only its [SPDX](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SPDX): `CeCILL-B`)

@LionelR Should we update update `setup.py` or `LICENSE` (and `version.py`)?

I noticed that some other projects have same "problem".
https://www.google.fr/search?q=Cecill-b+python+setup.py
https://github.com/d9pouces/django-floor
https://github.com/akaszynski/PyACVD
...

CeCILL-2.1 is a GPL compatible french license
CeCILL-B License is a BSD compatible french license (GPL incompatible)

https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Licence_CeCILL

CeCILL-B imposes author(s) citation not CeCILL-2.1

issue_title: Add syntax highlighting for DTC in Jupyter notebooks
issue_body:
#### Issue Checklist:
- [ ] Descriptive title
- [ ] Completed sections below

### Problem Description
Right now we're using `cat` to print DTCs in a Jupyter notebook. In order to increase readability we'd need to add syntax highlight for the YAML, and ideally for the python code in expression blocks.

### Solution Proposal

It is possible to print HTML using the `display()` function. It's also possible to execute Javascript code. We can create a `print_dtc()` function that loads a syntax highlighter, and then add markup to the parts of the DTC prior to calling display.

### Impact on current behavior
None

issue_title: Can we have an option to export static graph?
issue_body:
When using pytorch 0.4, export resnet to onnx, the FC layer will be something like

 %169 : Float(1, 512, 1, 1) = onnx::AveragePool[kernel_shape=[7, 7], pads=[0, 0, 0, 0], strides=[1, 1]](%168), scope: ResNet/AvgPool2d[avgpool]
  %170 : Float(1, 512) = onnx::Flatten[axis=1](%169), scope: ResNet
  %171 : Float(1, 6) = onnx::Gemm[alpha=1, beta=1, broadcast=1, transB=1](%170, %101, %102), scope: ResNet/Linear[fc]
  return (%171);

Latest version of pytorch save it as 
  %190 : Float(1, 512, 1, 1) = onnx::AveragePool[kernel_shape=[7, 7], pads=[0, 0, 0, 0], strides=[1, 1]](%189), scope: ResNet/AvgPool2d[avgpool]
  %191 : Dynamic = onnx::Shape(%190), scope: ResNet
  %192 : Dynamic = onnx::Slice[axes=[0], ends=[1], starts=[0]](%191), scope: ResNet
  %193 : Long() = onnx::Squeeze[axes=[0]](%192), scope: ResNet
  %194 : Long() = onnx::Constant[value={-1}](), scope: ResNet
  %195 : Dynamic = onnx::Unsqueeze[axes=[0]](%193), scope: ResNet
  %196 : Dynamic = onnx::Unsqueeze[axes=[0]](%194), scope: ResNet
  %197 : Dynamic = onnx::Concat[axis=0](%195, %196), scope: ResNet
  %198 : Float(1, 512) = onnx::Reshape(%190, %197), scope: ResNet
  %199 : Float(1, 6) = onnx::Gemm[alpha=1, beta=1, broadcast=1, transB=1](%198, %121, %122), scope: ResNet/Linear[fc]
  return (%199);

The new one actually caused trouble for us. For embedded device, we want fixed size, and let TensorRT optimize the graph for deployment.  I don't want the "nice" dynamic feature in onnx.

It is possible to put an option to latest pytorch, so that we can choose to export a static and simpler graph?

**问题**
如下图，我想把同学A和同学B写成两行，但是使用“\n”符号无效，请问有没有办法实现换行呢？


**运行环境（系统环境及 pyecharts 版本）**
win7，python3.6，pyecharts最新版


**代码及截图**
from pyecharts import Geo

def formatter_for_tootip(params):
    return params.value[2]

def plotmap():
    geo = Geo("G1115班本科毕业去向", "Made by YJT", title_color="#fff",
              title_pos="center", width=1366,
              height=768, background_color="#404a59")

    data = [('香港', '同学A 香港大学；同学B 香港科技大学')]
    attr, value = geo.cast(data)

    geo.add("", attr, value, type="effectScatter", is_random=True, maptype='world',
            effect_scale=5, tooltip_formatter=formatter_for_tootip,
            label_formatter="{b}")
    geo.render("test.html")

plotmap()

![default](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/40770755/42229831-fe6490e8-7f19-11e8-92ac-f5ae69f634b5.png)

issue_title: sr_cache
issue_body:
In C  the caching does not remove entries from cache except if they expire... if I well understood.
The only caveat with this is  if a file is deleted and recreated with same content... the cache will say
it was already processed... and the end result is the file is not re propagated.

in python,  there is a delete_path but it is really bad... it rewrites/clean the cache file...
so (1) could be removed from python and behave like in C
or (2) correct the python version and do it in C

This only affect sr_watch (or sr_cpost )

issue_title: subprocess file descriptor issues: sr sanity & sr audit fail or outputs disappearing.
issue_body:
Python recommendations have changed multiple times. There was os.system, subprocess.Popen, subprocess.check_call, and then subprocess.run.  when sr is invoked it causes instances to be started, and then the instances may in turn invoke other processes, such as sr_audit.  We end up with command output disappearing, in some cases.  note this:  https://docs.python.org/3/library/os.html#fd-inheritance which has changed things as well.

when we had subprocess.PIPE in start_instance, sr sanity would abort when invoked with run_command(), so I changed that to None, but worried that on older < 3.4.X the PIPE might be necessary... on the older version does sr sanity still crash?  dunno.  When the change is made,
sr sanity runs, (because I can see it restarting crashed instances) but there is no output.

issue_title: Questions for efsearch param, Thrift server
issue_body:
Hi,

I have a question about how/if a client of a Thrift query server using HNSW can set the efsearch query parameter per KNN query.

The low-level [KNNQuery](https://github.com/nmslib/nmslib/blob/d3cc0899604698f1e78867cf5396031268db55f1/similarity_search/include/knnquery.h#L36) class seems to take the efs parameter.

However, the Thrift query server does not seem to take it at [interface](https://github.com/nmslib/nmslib/blob/d1aba8d76894e34ace771e0624db7ebd50035357/query_server/protocol.thrift#L29) or at [implementation](https://github.com/nmslib/nmslib/blob/d3cc0899604698f1e78867cf5396031268db55f1/query_server/cpp_client_server/QueryService_server.cpp#L313)

Furthermore, I did some small tests on [KNNQuery](https://github.com/nmslib/nmslib/blob/d3cc0899604698f1e78867cf5396031268db55f1/similarity_search/include/knnquery.h#L36) and found the efs search parameter there did not affect results. Is it ignored/deprecated and therefore not included in the Thrift query server?

If the efs parameter in [KNNQuery](https://github.com/nmslib/nmslib/blob/d3cc0899604698f1e78867cf5396031268db55f1/similarity_search/include/knnquery.h#L36) is confirmed not to be working, are clients expected to set it at index using [SetQueryTimeParams](https://github.com/nmslib/nmslib/blob/d3cc0899604698f1e78867cf5396031268db55f1/similarity_search/include/method/multi_index.h#L58) before sending each query? I am worried that will cause various concurrency issues, from read/write error to race condition.

Thank you!

issue_title: Non-deterministic parser state for ambiguous example with priorities
issue_body:
I have a very small example of an ambiguity in a parser:
```python
from lark import Lark

l = Lark(
'''start: GENERIC | SPECIFIC
GENERIC.2: /\w+/
SPECIFIC.1: "HelloWorld"
''', parser="earley", lexer="dynamic", ambiguity="resolve"
)

for i in range(1, 101):
    print(l.parse("HelloWorld"))
```

Both GENERIC and SPECIFIC matches, but I have set a priority forcing GENERIC to match. This is not always the result though. If running this program multiple times it will match either GENERIC or SPECIFIC seemingly random. All matches for one run will be the same, but not between runs. This signals that a state in the parser is different between the runs.

issue_title: [REQUEST] Lowercase output for Author, Mentions and Hashtags
issue_body:
Hi,

Would you be able to lowercase the outputs for: -

- Tweet Author
- Mentions
- Hashtags

The reason is that some people use hashtags using a mixture of case, e.g. #Brexit / #brexit / #BREXIT. Or they refer to people in tweets in the different ways, e.g. @ThisAccount / @thisaccount

When manipulating the data in e.g. Excel, it makes life just that bit harder and longer ;) So it would be great if you could include this or make it an option.

Thanks!

issue_title: Bad HTTP content-negotation
issue_body:
pierre.a...@gmail.com, 2010-01-27T12:17:42.000Z 
When executing the following code:

  from rdflib import ConjunctiveGraph
  g = ConjunctiveGraph()
  g.parse("http://localhost:12345/foo", format="n3")

my localhost HTTP server receives the following request

  GET /foo HTTP/1.1
  Accept-Encoding: identity
  Host: localhost:12345
  Connection: close
  Accept: application/rdf+xml,application/xhtml+xml;q=0.5
  User-Agent: rdflib-2.4.0 (http://rdflib.net/; eikeon@eikeon.com)

The Accept: field is wrong: the agent should request 'text/n3'.

Even worse, since it does not even state that it accepts */*, the server is
not supposed to reply with *anything* else than rdf+xml or xhtml, even if
N3 was available for that resource.

And assuming that only N3 is available, "then the server SHOULD send a 406
(not acceptable) response" (per RFC2616), although many servers fail to do
so and reply with whatever content they have, regardless of the Accept field...
